mentions.note: Leading workforce development initiative (America's Workforce Academy) addressing skilled labor shortage; bullish on company vision and data center investment thesis.
Potential equity issuance to fund data-center capex could dilute shareholder base alongside Google and Amazon offerings, draining market liquidity pre-SpaceX IPO.
Considering colossal fund raise for AI ambitions (similar to Alphabet's multi-billion move). Supply of new stock flooding market is a headwind; investors must sell other holdings to fund purchases.

Powerful suite: Facebook, Instagram, eyeglasses, AI, WhatsApp; but not excelling visibly or printing money way it used to; spent big on talent, pulled multiple rabbits from hats; what have you done for me lately answer is nada; in what-have-you-done-for-lately business.
Major AI player and hyperscaler; benefits from Nvidia's booming data center business; investing heavily in infrastructure for AI revolution.
High-quality growth name (formerly Facebook) that performed well even during recession; example of company that works in thick or thin economic cycles.
AI models layer company. Insane capital spending on AI infrastructure, comparable to Eisenhower interstate highway system in scale.

33% revenue growth (fastest in 5 years). $56B revenue now vs. $29B in 2021 for similar growth rate. 3.5B daily active users; $150B capex proves data center business model. Mark Zuckerberg's track record warrants giving him benefit of doubt. Oversold on growth guidance moderation; fundamentals intact.
No cloud business to offset AI initiative losses from Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp. Spectacular traditional business sales ignored. Lacks earnings diversification of pure cloud players.
Earnings season strong across big tech save Meta; underperformed peer group this week.
33% revenue growth, 62% EPS growth but family DAP missed and shrank; CapEx raised $10B; investors unconvinced ad business justifies spend; down 8%.
Raised capex $10B despite 33% revenue growth; fastest growth in 5 years but Cramer wants more spend justification. Mixed signals on capital allocation.
Reporting Q1 earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); Cramer owns in trust; historically underperforms (clobbered Oct 29, dragged market down Jan 28); watch capital spending commitments.
Reports Wednesday; critical data point on AI capex returns. Has spent fortunes on data centers — will capital spending continue or pause?
10% layoff shows Zuckerberg's ruthless cost discipline ('chainsaw'); stock should have gone higher not lower.
Llama lagging but starting to show improvements; in middle tier of AI platforms; still has work to do vs. Claude/OpenAI.
Announced new AI model Muse Spark with positive reception; surged 4.4% today on top of big move yesterday. Talent investment in AI paying off.
Major AI infrastructure investor; benefiting from hardware dominance trend as it deploys Nvidia and other chips for AI.
Okay performance today; needs stronger follow-through; heavy data center CapEx justified by contract returns.
Trading 19x earnings after legal losses in New Mexico/LA courts; appeals expected to trim awards; First Amendment defense strong; market overreacting.
Two state court lawsuits ($375M NM, $6M CA) caused $200B cap loss; federal appeals likely overturn on First Amendment/Section 230; J&J talc precedent shows ultimate recovery.

Lost court cases on social media as tobacco narrative; stock down tens of billions but won't impact long-term earnings; interim pain comes before appeal reversal (like J&J case pattern); stay disciplined on fundamentals.
Added Nvidia partnership for AI; Zuckerberg understands token cost efficiency and force multiplier effect of Nvidia's inference architecture; heavy infrastructure investor.

Caller Robert raised question on Meta earnings coming next month. Cramer speculates Zuckerberg may embrace limited Dorsey-style workforce cuts (Square/fintech model) to go all-in on AI, creating shareholder fortunes. Conjecture on future strategy.

Huge top/bottom line beat; Mark Zuckerberg explained AI investments driving profitability in core ad business; 115-135B capex 2026 justified by guidance; slid lower post-earnings but quarter is free; own for Charitable Trust.
Terrific quarter but only narrative is advertising + Ray-Bans; management never promotes stock or defends thesis; indifferent on execution.

Mark Zuckerberg delivered first unequivocal end-user statement that AI is genuine profit multiplier for advertising; 3.5B DAU + AI-driven targeting = unstoppable. Rock in the rock-paper-scissors game.
Huge earnings beat and solid revenue beat; confident in 2026 capex ($135B AI buildout) despite ability to grow operating income; stock rallying furiously after hours.

Reports Wednesday. Core ad business strong but CapEx guidance will dominate reaction — 2026 spend forecast even higher than 2025. Need clarity on ROI from multi-billion-dollar AI investments. Stock down 16% from August highs; catalyst required for rally.
Terrible recently until today; Zuckerberg said he'd spend all he can on data center buildout. Need explanation of ROI on buildout beyond AI glasses.
Lost its way; P/E collapsed from 30x to 22x earnings on market skepticism of growth rate; earnings power questioned by OpenAI competition; still quality but watch carefully.
Down 8% YTD as money rotates out of quality mega-cap tech into speculative froth; high-quality company but losing to riskier bets.
Taiwan Semi call transcript indicates strong data center demand from hyperscalers; Meta's social media strength also noted as picking up.
Data center energy crunch; abandoning clean energy commitment could lift stock; needs cheaper power alternatives.

Up 13%, capex 70-72B 2025 likely rising to ~100B 2026; lacks clear AI strategy vs. Google/OpenAI, no cloud rival; dominant digital advertising (Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp); Cramer confident in Zuckerberg, owns big position; thinks 'power gating' limits actual spend vs. guidance.

Dina Pal McCormick running programs teaching trades (electrician, plumber, data center work) with Meta funding. Impressive social/community contribution. Preferred AR/VR play over Snap based on credibility and execution.
